key_insight: Gradient NiCr–Al detonation-sprayed coatings outperformed homogeneous coatings in high-temperature oxidation resistance by maintaining a more stable protective oxide layer.
engineering_relevance: For thermal power plant components, grading the coating chemistry improves durability by reducing oxidation-driven degradation and extending service life under harsh operating conditions.
method: Detonation-sprayed gradient and homogeneous NiCr–Al composite coatings
High-temperature oxidation testing under real thermal power plant conditions
SEM and phase/compositional analysis of oxide growth and elemental redistribution
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that gradient coatings retained structural integrity, showed more uniform Al/Cr distribution, and formed adherent Al2O3/Cr2O3 scales, while homogeneous coatings suffered Al depletion, cracking, and spallation.
watch_item: Performance was demonstrated in one operating environment, so transferability to other temperatures, atmospheres, or component geometries should be validated before deployment.
paper_title: Investigation of Detonation-Sprayed NiCr–Al Coatings Tested under Real Operating Conditions of a Thermal Power Plant
